title: Add package-working-directory and push policy controls to reusable semantic-release workflow
description: Update platform-ci semantic-release-reusable workflow to support monorepo package config (python/pyproject.toml) via configurable working directory and to support protected-branch-safe push controls. Then update Kanbus workflow to use these inputs and remove root workaround.
creator: derek.norrbom

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Starting implementation: patch platform-ci reusable workflow with package working_directory and push controls, then update Kanbus consumer workflow and remove root pyproject workaround.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Implemented proper cross-repo fix. platform-ci reusable workflow now supports: (a) working_directory input to load package-local semantic-release config in monorepos, (b) push_changes input to disable direct push when branch protection forbids it, (c) publish gating on push_changes, and (d) robust version extraction from non-v-prefixed tags (e.g., kanbus-py-0.17.0). Kanbus now consumes reusable with working_directory=python and push_changes=false, keeping config in python/pyproject.toml.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Verified reusable semantic-release consumers. Only Kanbus currently uses build_command='python -m build' without self-installing build; Biblicus and Tactus self-install build in command, Virtuus does not use python -m build, Plexus has no build_command. Applying long-term fix in platform-ci reusable workflow to install build by default.
